news.title: Apple now makes one in four iPhones in India: report
news.summary: Apple reportedly produced 55 million units in India last year, a shift accelerated as Apple faced growing uncertainty in China.

news.title: Yann LeCun’s AMI Labs raises $1.03 billion to build world models
news.summary: AMI Labs, the new venture cofounded by Turing Prize winner Yann LeCun after he left Meta, has raised $1.03 billion at a $3.5 billion pre-money valuation.

news.title: An iPhone-hacking toolkit used by Russian spies likely came from U.S military contractor
news.summary: Google found a series of hacking tools they said were used by a Russian espionage group and a cybercriminal group in China. Sources from a U.S. government defense contractor said some of those hacking tools were theirs.

news.title: Electric air taxi maker Archer hits back at Joby in countersuit alleging concealed Chinese ties
news.summary: Archer's countersuit comes four months after Joby sued its rival for alleged 'corporate espionage.'

news.title: Founders Fund nears $6B close for latest growth fund, sources say
news.summary: The fresh fundraise comes less than a year after Founders Fund closed its third growth fund, a $4.6 billion vehicle intended primarily for follow-on investments in its successful late-stage companies.

news.title: Apple smart home display rumors now point to a fall launch with iOS 27
news.summary: The rumored "HomePod with a screen" we've heard so much about was reportedly lined up for launch in 2025, and then this spring, and now, according to the latest updates, it's on the shelf until this fall. news.title: The government shutdown is hitting airports — but not ICE
news.summary: Chaos reigned at airports across the country last weekend, with thousands of travelers reportedly waiting in hours-long security lines thanks to staffing shortages. Transportation Security Administration (TSA) and Coast Guard workers have turned to food banks for assistance after weeks without pay.

news.title: Everything from the last week of everything is gambling now
news.summary: Kalshi is facing backlash from its own users after a class action lawsuit was filed last week alleging it failed to pay out as promised for wagers regarding when Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah ​Ali Khamenei would leave or be removed from office. news.title: Employees across OpenAI and Google support Anthropic’s lawsuit against the Pentagon
news.summary: On Monday, Anthropic filed its lawsuit against the Department of Defense over being designated as a supply chain risk. news.title: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber will step aside
news.summary: Jay Graber became the first CEO of Bluesky in 2021 when the network and its AT Protocol spun out from life as a Twitter research project to go independent, but now she's leaving that role, as reported previously by Wired. news.title: After falling far behind the rest of industry, Blue Origin creates new stock option plan
news.summary: "It's a big fat middle finger for those that thought they had something."

news.title: Quad Cortex mini amp modeler: All the power, half the size
news.summary: A warehouse of guitar gear in the palm of your hand.

news.title: Testing Apple's 2026 16-inch MacBook Pro, M5 Max, and its new "performance" cores
news.summary: M5 Pro Max's "performance" CPU cores definitely aren't just rebranded E-cores.

news.title: US blindsides states with surprise settlement in Live Nation/Ticketmaster trial
news.summary: States seek mistrial, saying "sudden disappearance" of US will influence jury.

news.title: An unlikely set of clues helps reconstruct ancient Chinese disasters
news.summary: Shang Dynasty oracle bones and modern weather models feature in the same study.

news.title: Yann LeCun Raises $1 Billion to Build AI That Understands the Physical World
news.summary: Meta’s former chief AI scientist has long argued that human-level AI will come from mastering the physical world, not language. His new startup, AMI, aims to prove it.

news.title: Nvidia Is Planning to Launch an Open-Source AI Agent Platform
news.summary: Ahead of its annual developer conference, Nvidia is readying a new approach to software that embraces AI agents similar to OpenClaw.

news.title: ‘Flying Cars’ Will Take Off in American Skies This Summer
news.summary: The federal government announced a new pilot program designed to get new kinds of ultralight vehicles and “eVTOLs” up and running around the country—even if they're not fully FAA-certified.

news.title: Anthropic Claims Pentagon Feud Could Cost It Billions
news.summary: Executives at the AI startup say companies paused deal talks after the Trump administration labeled it a supply-chain risk, warning that the fallout could cause a major revenue hit.

news.title: OpenAI and Google Workers File Amicus Brief in Support of Anthropic Against the US Government
news.summary: Google DeepMind chief scientist Jeff Dean is among the AI researchers and engineers rushing to Anthropic's defense.
